expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns in the codebase or skill instructions instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response uses generic placeholders like `src/<cli_package>/...` and user notes explicitly say it "assumed a generic Python CLI layout" because repo-specific paths were not provided. No concrete codebase or skill-instruction pattern lookup is evidenced in the final response.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
expectations.evidence: Final response provides an exact artifact path (`/home/adam/.agents/.agents/scratchpad/config-validation-module/handoff-plan.md`), and that file exists with full handoff content.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: Artifact contains explicit sections `Implementation Slices`, `File-by-File Implementation Map`, and `Validation Plan` in a structured handoff template format.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.
expectations.evidence: Artifact header fields are concretely set: `Status: ready for implementation`, `Track: Standard`, `Recommended Next Step: implement directly` with no bracket placeholders in these fields.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.
expectations.evidence: Every `Relevant Findings` bullet pairs a file path with purpose (e.g., entrypoint fail-fast location, config source-of-truth, test coverage role, tooling alignment).

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.
expectations.evidence: Artifact has one bounded open question (exit-code convention) and includes explicit risk framing: `Risk if wrong: inconsistent automation behavior or mismatch with current scripting expectations.`

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: Final response includes an `Artifact Status` section and states the exact path to the handoff artifact.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: `Recommended Design` explains why centralized startup validation is preferred over scattered command checks (uniform behavior, less duplication, easier testing), which is explicit rationale/trade-off framing.

expectations.text: The Validation Plan section of the handoff artifact contains TDD completion, a code-simplifier refactor pass, and independent code-reviewer review as named discrete steps — not buried solely in the Implementation Slices section.
expectations.evidence: `Validation Plan` explicitly lists: `TDD red-green-refactor completion`, `Code-simplifier refactor pass`, and `Independent code-reviewer review` as separate bullets.

claims.claim: A concrete handoff artifact file was created and referenced with an exact path.
claims.evidence: Declared path appears in final response and the file exists at that location with populated content.

claims.claim: The handoff artifact includes all required planning sections for implementation handoff quality.
claims.evidence: Artifact includes reusable planning structure including findings, slices, file map, and validation workflow controls.

claims.claim: The run performed repository- or skill-pattern discovery before proposing design details.
claims.evidence: User notes indicate a generic assumed layout and placeholders; no concrete repo/skill lookup evidence appears in the response.

uncertainties: User notes say repo-specific paths were not available and placeholders were used, reducing confidence in codebase-grounded mapping.
needs_review: Whether this eval should require explicit evidence of codebase/skill discovery when the prompt context lacks concrete repo file paths.
workarounds: Run copied the handoff artifact into the outputs directory for grader visibility while also reporting the workflow artifact path.

suggestions.assertion: Looks for relevant patterns in the codebase or skill instructions instead of inventing the design in isolation.
suggestions.reason: Require explicit mention of discovered patterns (e.g., named files inspected or cited workflow clauses) so this can be unambiguously scored as pass/fail.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong handoff artifact quality and workflow compliance on structure, metadata, and validation rigor. Single miss is lack of explicit discovery evidence for repo/skill pattern lookup.
